w:r.w:t: Read all of this leaflet carefully before you start taking this medicine because it contains important information for you.

If you get any side effects,

capsules are and what it is used for
Panadol 500 mg capsules can be used for headache including migraine and tension
pain, back pain, muscle pain, period pain, sore throat, fever, aches
flu, pain due to mild arthritis.
if you have ever had an allergic reaction
if you have severe illnesses, including severe renal impairment or sepsis (when bacteria and their toxins circulate in the blood leading to organ damage), suffer from malnutrition, chronic
A serious condition called metabolic acidosis (a blood
and fluid abnormality) has been reported in patients in these situations when paracetamol is used at
regular doses for a prolonged period
together with flucloxacillin. Symptoms may include: serious
deep rapid breathing, drowsiness, feeling sick (nausea) and being sick
if you get a combination of these symptoms. You may need to avoid using this product altogether or limit the amount of paracetamol that you take.

can worsen the headache. If this occurs,

If you are pregnant or breast- feeding, think you may be pregnant or are planning to
pharmacist for advice before taking this medicine.
Panadol 500 mg capsules can be used during pregnancy if necessary. You should use the lowest possible dose that reduces
time possible. Contact your doctor if the pain and/or fever are

the recommended dose for a short
This medicine contains 108 mg sorbitol liquid in each soft capsule. Sorbitol is a source of fructose.
told you that you (or your child) have an intolerance to some sugars or if you have been diagnosed with hereditary
genetic disorder in which a person cannot break down fructose, talk to your doctor before you (or your child) take or receive this medicine.
mg propylene glycol in each soft
Always take this medicine exactly as described in this leaflet or as your doctor or
pharmacist if you are not sure.
at a time, if necessary, repeat every 4 to 6
paracetamol for children should be based on the age.
than 3 days at a time unless your doctor tells you to. Do not give to children under 10 years.

and take it for the shortest time possible.
dose. This could lead to damage to the liver (see section 4).

If your symptoms continue or your headache becomes persistent,
If you suddenly stop taking Panadol 500 mg capsules,
If you have any further questions on the use of this medicine, ask your doctor
medicine can cause side effects, although not everybody gets them.
of serious skin reactions have been reported.
may be severe such as skin rash and itching sometimes with swelling
These are more likely if you have experienced them before when
condition that can make blood more acidic, see Section 2).
If you get any side effects, talk to your doctor or pharmacist. This includes any possible side effects not listed in this leaflet. You can also report side
effects directly via the Yellow Card Scheme at
yellowcard or search for MHRA Yellow Card in the Google Play or Apple App Store. By
reporting side effects you can help provide more information on the safety of this medicine.
the sight and reach of children.
Do not use this medicine after the expiry date which is stated on the packaging after “EXP”.
Do not throw away any medicines via wastewater or household waste. Ask your pharmacist how to throw
away medicines you no longer use. These measures will help protect the environment.
water, propylene glycol and silica (colloidal anhydrous).
sorbitol (liquid, partially dehydrated), purified water, titanium dioxide (E171).
